seqkit.description: SeqKit - A cross-platform and ultrafast toolkit for FASTA/Q file manipulation

stats.description: Compute basic sequence statistics: count, total length, average length, GC content

grep.description: Search and extract sequences matching a sequence ID or regex pattern

pattern.description: Sequence ID or regex pattern to match, e.g. 'contig_1' or 'contig_\d+'

seq.description: Convert or filter sequences (reverse complement, remove line wrapping, etc.)

head.description: Extract the first N sequences from a FASTA/Q file

count.description: Number of sequences to extract, e.g. 10

blast.description: BLAST+ - Basic Local Alignment Search Tool for sequence similarity search
blast.notes: Standard workflow: run makeblastdb first to build the database, then run blastn/blastp. Database files are written to /work/.

blastn.description: Nucleotide sequence similarity search against a BLAST database

db.description: Path prefix of the BLAST nucleotide database, e.g. /work/mydb (created by makeblastdb)

output.description: Output filename for tabular results (-outfmt 6), e.g. results.txt

blastn.notes: db must be the path prefix generated by makeblastdb

blastp.description: Protein sequence similarity search against a BLAST database

db.description: Path prefix of the BLAST protein database, e.g. /work/mydb (created by makeblastdb -dbtype prot)

output.description: Output filename for tabular results, e.g. results.txt

blastp.notes: db must be the path prefix generated by makeblastdb

makeblastdb.description: Build a local BLAST database from a FASTA file

type.description: Database molecule type: nucl (nucleotide) or prot (protein)

db.description: Database name prefix (no path), e.g. mydb. Output will be written to /work/mydb

makeblastdb.notes: The generated database path is /work/{db}. Use this path as the db parameter in blastn/blastp.

prodigal.description: Prodigal - Prokaryotic gene prediction for complete and draft genomes
prodigal.notes: Use predict for complete genomes; use meta for assembled metagenome contigs

predict.description: Predict genes from a complete prokaryotic genome; outputs protein and nucleotide sequences

protein.description: Output filename for predicted protein sequences, e.g. genes.faa

nucleotide.description: Output filename for predicted nucleotide sequences, e.g. genes.fna

predict.notes: For metagenome-assembled contigs use the meta operation instead

meta.description: Predict genes in metagenomic mode (-p meta); suitable for short assembled contigs

protein.description: Output filename for predicted protein sequences, e.g. genes.faa

meta.notes: The -p meta flag is built into the command

hmmer.description: HMMER - Biosequence analysis using profile hidden Markov models
hmmer.notes: HMM database files must be indexed with hmmpress before running hmmscan. Place local HMM files in runtime/bio_tools/hmmer/.

hmmscan.description: Search protein sequences against an HMM database (sequence → database direction)

db.description: Absolute path to the HMM database file (must be pre-indexed with hmmpress), e.g. /path/to/Pfam-A.hmm

hmmscan.notes: input must be a protein FASTA file; db must have been processed with hmmpress

hmmsearch.description: Search an HMM profile against a protein sequence database (model → sequences direction)

db.description: Absolute path to the HMM model file, e.g. /path/to/model.hmm

hmmsearch.notes: input must be a protein FASTA sequence database

hmmpress.description: Build binary index files for an HMM file; required before running hmmscan

db.description: HMM filename relative to runtime/bio_tools/hmmer/, e.g. Pfam-A.hmm

hmmpress.notes: db must be an HMM file already uploaded to runtime/bio_tools/hmmer/

hmmbuild.description: Build an HMM profile from a multiple sequence alignment file

output.description: Output HMM model filename, e.g. myfamily.hmm

hmmbuild.notes: input must be a multiple sequence alignment file (FASTA/Stockholm/Clustal/Phylip format)

dorado.description: Dorado - Oxford Nanopore basecalling and demultiplexing tool
dorado.notes: Requires GPU support (NVIDIA Container Toolkit). Standard workflow: basecall then demux.

basecall.description: Basecall raw Nanopore signals (pod5/fast5) into FASTQ reads

model.description: Basecalling model name matching your flow cell, e.g. dna_r10.4.1_e8.2.260bps_sup@v4.1.0

kit.description: Sequencing kit name, e.g. SQK-RBK001 or SQK-LSK114

basecall.notes: input should be a pod5 or fast5 directory

demux.description: Demultiplex barcoded reads by barcode identity

kit.description: Barcoding kit name, e.g. SQK-RBK001 or SQK-NBD114-96

demux.notes: input should be the BAM or FASTQ output from basecall

nanoplot.description: NanoPlot - Quality visualization and statistics for Nanopore sequencing data

basic.description: Generate Nanopore read quality statistics plots and an HTML summary report

prefix.description: Prefix for output filenames, e.g. sample1

basic.notes: input must be a FASTQ file; output includes an HTML report and quality plots

minimap2.description: Minimap2 - Fast sequence alignment for long and short reads
minimap2.notes: preset options: map-pb (PacBio CLR), map-ont (ONT), map-hifi (HiFi), sr (Illumina short reads)

map.description: Align reads to a reference genome and output in SAM format

preset.description: Alignment preset: map-pb (PacBio CLR), map-ont (ONT), map-hifi (PacBio HiFi), or sr (Illumina short reads)

reference.description: Absolute path to the reference genome FASTA file

query.description: Absolute path to the query reads file (FASTA or FASTQ)

filter.description: Align ONT reads to a reference and output SAM (use samtools downstream to filter unmapped reads)

reference.description: Absolute path to the reference genome FASTA file

filter.notes: Preset is fixed to map-ont; use samtools view -f 4 to extract unmapped reads afterward

samtools.description: SAMtools - Utilities for manipulating SAM/BAM/CRAM alignment files
samtools.notes: Standard workflow: view (convert) → sort → index → stats

view_filter_unmapped.description: Extract unmapped reads (-f 4) from a BAM file and output as gzipped FASTQ
view_filter_unmapped.notes: input must be a SAM or BAM file; -f 4 selects reads that failed to map

view.notes: input must be a SAM file; -b flag is built in to output BAM

sort.description: Sort a BAM file by genomic coordinates

output.description: Output sorted BAM filename, e.g. sorted.bam

index.description: Build a .bai index for a coordinate-sorted BAM file
index.notes: input must be a sorted BAM file; the index file is written as input.bam.bai automatically

stats.description: Generate alignment quality statistics for a SAM/BAM file

flye.description: Flye - De novo assembler for long reads (ONT, PacBio, HiFi); supports metagenome mode
flye.notes: Use meta mode for metagenome assembly; final assembly is written to assembly.fasta in the output directory

meta.description: Assemble a metagenome from ONT reads using metagenomic mode

output.description: Output directory name, e.g. flye_meta_output. Final assembly is assembly.fasta inside this directory.

meta.notes: input must be ONT reads in FASTQ format; --nano-raw and --meta flags are built in

ont.description: Assemble a genome from ONT reads

hifi.description: Assemble a genome from PacBio HiFi reads

seqtk.description: Seqtk - Lightweight toolkit for processing FASTA/Q sequence files

sample.description: Randomly subsample sequences from a FASTA/Q file

fraction.description: Subsampling fraction (0-1, e.g. 0.1 for 10%) or an integer number of reads to sample (e.g. 1000)

size.description: Count the number of sequences and total bases in a FASTA/Q file

megahit.description: MEGAHIT - Ultra-fast and memory-efficient assembler for metagenomics short reads

assemble.description: Assemble a metagenome from paired-end Illumina reads

r1.description: Absolute path to forward reads file (.fastq/.fq/.gz)

r2.description: Absolute path to reverse reads file (.fastq/.fq/.gz)

output.description: Output directory name, e.g. megahit_output. Final contigs are in final.contigs.fa.

bakta.description: Bakta - Rapid and comprehensive annotation tool for bacterial and archaeal genomes
bakta.notes: Database is located at /home/zczhao/GAgent/data/databases/bio_tools/bakta_db and mounted as /work/database inside the container

annotate.description: Annotate a bacterial/archaeal genome and output GFF3, GenBank, and other formats

prefix.description: Prefix for all output filenames, e.g. sample1

annotate.notes: input must be a genome FASTA file; the database is mounted automatically

metabat2.description: MetaBAT2 - Metagenome binning tool using tetranucleotide frequency and coverage
metabat2.notes: Standard workflow: run depth first to generate a coverage depth file, then run bin

bin.description: Bin contigs into draft genomes using coverage depth and sequence composition

contigs.description: Absolute path to the contigs FASTA file

depth.description: Absolute path to the depth file generated by the depth operation (jgi_summarize_bam_contig_depths)

output_prefix.description: Output bin filename prefix, e.g. bin. Output files will be bin.1.fa, bin.2.fa, etc.

depth.description: Calculate per-contig sequencing coverage depth from BAM files (prerequisite for bin operation)

bam_files.description: Absolute path(s) to sorted BAM file(s); separate multiple files with commas

concoct.description: CONCOCT - Metagenome binning using coverage and composition
concoct.notes: Requires a pre-generated coverage table in TSV format

bin.description: Bin contigs using coverage depth table and tetranucleotide frequencies

coverage.description: Coverage table filename (TSV, relative to work directory), e.g. coverage.tsv

contigs.description: Absolute path to the contigs FASTA file

maxbin2.description: MaxBin2 - Metagenome binning using expectation-maximization algorithm
maxbin2.notes: Requires an abundance file with one coverage value per contig per line

bin.description: Bin contigs using sequencing abundance (coverage) values

contigs.description: Absolute path to the contigs FASTA file

output.description: Output bin filename prefix, e.g. maxbin. Output files will be maxbin.001.fasta, etc.

abundance.description: Absolute path to the abundance file (one coverage value per line, matching contigs order)

das_tool.description: DAS Tool - Dereplication and aggregation of metagenome-assembled genomes from multiple binners
das_tool.notes: Integrates results from MetaBAT2, CONCOCT, MaxBin2, etc. and selects the best non-redundant bins

integrate.description: Integrate multiple binning tool results and output an optimized set of bins

bins.description: Comma-separated paths to scaffold-to-bin TSV files from each binner, e.g. /path/metabat.tsv,/path/concoct.tsv

contigs.description: Absolute path to the contigs FASTA file

checkm.description: CheckM - Assess the quality (completeness and contamination) of genome bins
checkm.notes: Database is located at /home/zczhao/GAgent/data/databases/bio_tools/checkm_data and mounted as /work/database

lineage_wf.description: Run the full CheckM lineage workflow to assess completeness and contamination of bins

output.description: Output summary table filename, e.g. checkm_results.tsv

output_dir.description: CheckM working directory name, e.g. checkm_output

lineage_wf.notes: input must be the absolute path to a directory containing bin FASTA files (.fa suffix); database is mounted automatically

qa.description: Summarize bin quality results from a prior lineage_wf run

tree.description: Path to the lineage.ms file generated by lineage_wf (relative to work directory)

input.description: Path to the marker file from the lineage_wf output directory

gtdbtk.description: GTDB-Tk - Taxonomic classification of genome bins using the GTDB database
gtdbtk.notes: Database is located at /home/zczhao/GAgent/data/databases/bio_tools/gtdbtk and mounted as /work/database

classify_wf.description: Classify genome bins against the GTDB r220 reference database

genome_dir.description: Absolute path to the directory containing bin FASTA files (must use .fa extension)

classify_wf.notes: Database is mounted automatically; -x fa specifies the .fa file extension for genome bins

genomad.description: geNomad - Identification and annotation of viral and plasmid sequences
genomad.notes: Database (~5 GB) is located at /home/zczhao/GAgent/data/databases/bio_tools/genomad and mounted as /work/database

end_to_end.description: Run the full geNomad pipeline: annotation, virus/plasmid classification, and provirus detection
end_to_end.notes: input must be a genome or metagenome contigs FASTA file; database is mounted automatically

annotate.description: Run only the sequence annotation step of the geNomad pipeline

find_proviruses.description: Detect integrated prophages within bacterial genome sequences

checkv.description: CheckV - Quality assessment of metagenome-assembled viral genomes
checkv.notes: Database (~2 GB) is located at /home/zczhao/GAgent/data/databases/bio_tools/checkv/checkv-db-v1.5 and mounted as /work/database

end_to_end.description: Run the full CheckV pipeline to assess viral genome completeness (recommended)
end_to_end.notes: input must be a viral sequences FASTA file; database is mounted automatically

complete_genomes.description: Identify complete viral genomes by detecting direct or inverted terminal repeats (DTR/ITR)

virsorter2.description: VirSorter2 - Viral sequence discovery from metagenome-assembled contigs
virsorter2.notes: Database (~10-15 GB) is located at /home/zczhao/GAgent/data/databases/bio_tools/virsorter2/db/db and mounted as /work/database

run.description: Identify viral sequences from metagenome contigs

min_score.description: Minimum virus score threshold (0-1), default 0.5. Lower values increase recall; higher values increase precision.

run.notes: input must be a contigs FASTA file; database is mounted automatically

bwa.description: BWA - Burrows-Wheeler Aligner for short read sequences
bwa.notes: Standard workflow: run bwa index first, then bwa mem for alignment

index.description: Build a BWA index for a reference genome (required before alignment)
index.notes: input must be a reference genome FASTA file; generates .amb .ann .bwt .pac .sa index files automatically

mem.description: Align reads to a reference genome using BWA-MEM algorithm; outputs SAM format

reference.description: Absolute path to the reference genome FASTA file (must have been indexed with bwa index)

mem.notes: input must be a reads FASTQ file

bowtie2.description: Bowtie 2 - Fast and sensitive short read alignment tool
bowtie2.notes: Standard workflow: run bowtie2-build first to build the index, then align

build.description: Build a Bowtie2 index from a reference genome FASTA file

index_prefix.description: Index filename prefix, e.g. my_index. Generates my_index.1.bt2 through my_index.rev.2.bt2.

align.description: Align paired-end reads to a reference genome; outputs SAM format

index.description: Path prefix of the Bowtie2 index (generated by build), e.g. /work/my_index

r1.description: Absolute path to forward reads FASTQ file

r2.description: Absolute path to reverse reads FASTQ file

mmseqs2.description: MMseqs2 - Ultra-fast protein and nucleotide sequence search and clustering
mmseqs2.notes: Supports easy-search, easy-linclust, easy-taxonomy and other modes

easy_search.description: Sequence similarity search (BLAST-like but much faster)

query.description: Absolute path to the query sequences FASTA file

target.description: Absolute path to the target database FASTA file

output.description: Output filename in tabular format, e.g. results.m8

search_type.description: Search type: 1 = protein vs protein, 3 = nucleotide vs nucleotide (default 3)

easy_linclust.description: Linear-time sequence clustering for sequence dereplication

min_seq_id.description: Minimum sequence identity threshold (0-1), default 0.9 (90% identity)

trim_galore.description: Trim Galore! - Quality control and adapter trimming for Illumina sequencing reads
trim_galore.notes: Auto-detects Illumina/Nextera/Small RNA adapters; wraps Cutadapt and FastQC

trim.description: Trim adapters and low-quality bases from single-end reads

quality.description: Phred quality score threshold for 3' end trimming, default 20

min_length.description: Minimum read length to keep after trimming (bp), default 20

trim.notes: input must be a single-end FASTQ file; adapters are detected automatically

trim_paired.description: Trim adapters and low-quality bases from paired-end reads while maintaining read pairing

r1.description: Absolute path to forward reads FASTQ file

r2.description: Absolute path to reverse reads FASTQ file

quality.description: Phred quality score threshold for 3' end trimming, default 20

min_length.description: Minimum read length to keep after trimming (bp), default 20

ngmlr.description: NGMLR - Long read aligner optimized for detecting structural variants
ngmlr.notes: Designed for ONT/PacBio long reads; recommended upstream tool for Sniffles2 SV calling

map.description: Align long reads to a reference genome and output in SAM format

reference.description: Absolute path to the reference genome FASTA file

preset.description: Sequencing platform preset: ont (Oxford Nanopore) or pacbio (PacBio), default ont

map.notes: input must be a FASTQ file; pipe the output SAM through samtools sort and index before running Sniffles2

sniffles2.description: Sniffles2 - Structural variant calling from long read alignments
sniffles2.notes: Requires a coordinate-sorted and indexed BAM file as input

call.description: Call structural variants (SVs) from a long read BAM file and output VCF

reference.description: Absolute path to the reference genome FASTA file

call.notes: input must be a coordinate-sorted BAM file with a .bai index

fastani.description: FastANI - Fast whole-genome Average Nucleotide Identity (ANI) estimation
fastani.notes: ANI >= 95% is the standard species boundary threshold; ~1000x faster than BLAST-based ANI

compare.description: Compute the ANI between a query genome and a reference genome

query.description: Absolute path to the query genome FASTA file

reference.description: Absolute path to the reference genome FASTA file

vibrant.description: VIBRANT - Virus identification using HMM-based annotation of metagenome contigs
vibrant.notes: Uses pre-trained HMM models; no external database download required

run.description: Identify and annotate viral sequences from metagenome contigs
run.notes: input must be a contigs FASTA file; sequences shorter than 1 kb are recommended to be filtered out first

iphop.description: iPHoP - Integrated Phage Host Prediction for metagenome-derived phages
iphop.notes: Database is large (~20 GB+); confirm the database is ready before running

predict.description: Predict bacterial hosts for phage sequences
predict.notes: input must be a viral sequences FASTA file; database is mounted automatically

pipeline.description: Path to a .nf pipeline script or an nf-core pipeline name, e.g. nf-core/rnaseq

run.notes: Work directory is fixed to /work/work; append -resume to continue from a previous run

clean.description: Remove cached intermediate files from the Nextflow work directory

snakemake.description: Snakemake - Python-based scientific workflow management system
snakemake.notes: Supports Conda environments for reproducible analyses

cores.description: Number of CPU cores to use; defaults to all available cores

run.notes: Working directory is fixed to /work; append --use-conda or --use-singularity as needed

dry_run.description: Preview the execution plan without running any jobs (dry-run mode)
